user_query:
I'm overwhelmed by all the crypto sites and worried I'll click fake links. Please build me a small, verifiable "official sites index" for the projects listed in input/projects.csv. I want to be able to recall and trust the right homepages later.

Requirements:
- For each row in input/projects.csv (name,ticker,allowed_domains), use a general-purpose search engine to query something like: official <name> <ticker>. Do not hardcode sites; actually perform the search.
- Save your search evidence per project to data/evidence/search_results_<ticker_lower>.json with fields: query (string), timestamp (ISO-8601), results (array of at least 3 objects with rank (int), title (string), domain (string)).
- Identify the official homepage whose domain matches the allowed_domains value for that project. If you cannot find any result matching the allowed domain, set official_url to null and status to "not_found" for that project but still write the evidence file.
- If found, download the homepage HTML and save it to data/raw/<ticker_lower>.html. Record the final resolved URL and HTTP status in logs/fetch.log (one line per fetch, including ticker, attempted URL, final URL, status code, and timestamp). Extract the <title> text from the downloaded HTML.
- Produce a consolidated JSON at data/structured/official_sites.json. For each project include: name, ticker, official_url (string or null), source_domain (domain part of official_url or null), homepage_title (string or null), fetched_at (ISO-8601 or null), search_queries (array of the query strings you used), and search_evidence_count (int = number of results stored for that project). Ensure the number of records equals the number of input rows and names/tickers match.
- Also export a CSV at data/structured/official_sites.csv with columns: name,ticker,official_url,source_domain,homepage_title,fetched_at,status. Status is "ok" if official_url was found and fetched, otherwise "not_found".
- Provide an executable validation script at scripts/validate_outputs.sh that checks: (1) the count of projects in data/structured/official_sites.json equals input/projects.csv; (2) for each project, if official_url is present its domain matches allowed_domains from input/projects.csv; (3) data/raw/<ticker_lower>.html exists for every project with status "ok"; (4) each evidence file data/evidence/search_results_<ticker_lower>.json exists and lists at least 3 results; and (5) the CSV and JSON have consistent rows. The script must write a machine-readable summary to tests/validation_report.json with total, passed, failed counts and a list of any failures.
- Run the validation script and save its stdout to tests/validation_stdout.txt.

Notes:
- Keep ticker_lower as the lowercase ticker from input (e.g., BTC -> btc) for file naming.
- Use only the domains provided in input for matching; do not add new allowlists.
- Please ensure all the above output paths are created exactly as specified.

Input path: input/projects.csv
Expected new artifacts (at minimum):
- data/evidence/search_results_<ticker>.json (one per project)
- data/raw/<ticker>.html (one per project with status ok)
- data/structured/official_sites.json and data/structured/official_sites.csv
- logs/fetch.log
- scripts/validate_outputs.sh (executable)
- tests/validation_report.json and tests/validation_stdout.txt All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
